scenarios.query:
Audit this docs parity scan:
```text
README: setup section says `docker compose up` — compose file deleted in March,
  replaced by `make dev`; badge links to CI that was migrated
API docs: document DELETE /v2/teams/{id} — endpoint removed two releases ago;
  new POST /v2/teams/{id}/archive undocumented
docstring: send_invoice(to, cc=None) — docs describe parameter `bcc` (never existed)
  and claim it raises InvoiceError; it actually returns an error result object
```
expected_behavior: Flags the broken README setup instructions (deleted compose file) as an onboarding regression
Flags the contradictory API docs: a documented endpoint that no longer exists and a new endpoint with no docs
Flags the docstring describing a nonexistent parameter and wrong error behavior as worse than no docs

scenarios.query:
Audit this scan:
```text
new feature shipped last month: scheduled exports (cron jobs, a queue, an alert)
  - tutorial written (how to click through the UI)
  - no reference for the 6 new config keys
  - no runbook: on-call has no detect/diagnose/rollback steps (paged twice already)
ADRs: none for the new queue dependency (RabbitMQ added; no decision record)
```
expected_behavior: Flags the missing reference docs for the new config keys (a tutorial is not a substitute for reference)
Flags the missing runbook for the new operational surface, citing the on-call pages
Flags the missing ADR for the architecturally significant new dependency

scenarios.query:
Audit this docs parity scan:
```text
README quickstart: CI-checked, green
public surface: 17/17 functions documented; docstring/signature parity green
CHANGELOG: current through last release, semver-categorized
runbooks: one per scheduled job, last reviewed this quarter
```
expected_behavior: Recognizes parity between surface and docs, runnable examples, current changelog
Does not demand more documentation volume when parity and accuracy are met
